Title: Set the Godot project root
Summary: Two paths to point Bleepforge at your Godot project: the first-run welcome screen on packaged builds, or an env var for dev.
Body:
## Why this matters

Bleepforge can't read or write `.tres` files, watch for changes, or render asset paths without knowing which Godot project to point at. The packaged AppImage / Windows installer shows a welcome screen on first run that prompts you to pick a folder; the dev CLI (`pnpm dev`) fails fast at boot if no root is configured. Either way, the goal is the same: tell Bleepforge where your project lives before it tries to do anything else.

## Resolution order

The server checks two places, in this order:

1. `data/preferences.json` for a `godotProjectRoot` field. This is what both the [Preferences page](/preferences) AND the first-run welcome screen write when you save a path.
2. The `GODOT_PROJECT_ROOT` environment variable. Bootstrap fallback for dev work before `preferences.json` exists.

If both are set, Preferences wins.

## First-run packaged builds — the welcome screen

When you launch a packaged AppImage or Windows installer with no `preferences.json` yet, the server boots in "limp mode" (no reconcile, no watcher, no asset cache — it listens and that's all). The renderer detects the empty root and shows a centered welcome screen instead of the broken-looking app shell.

Click **Pick game folder**, browse to the directory containing `project.godot`, and Bleepforge validates the pick inline before letting you apply. On apply, `preferences.json` is written and the app restarts so the server captures the new root at next boot.

## Dev workflow — env var or Preferences

For `pnpm dev` you can either set `GODOT_PROJECT_ROOT` in a `.env` file at the repo root, or use the Preferences page once the app boots. The dev CLI fails fast (instead of limp-moding) if no root is configured — easier to spot a misconfiguration than to wonder why nothing renders.

> warn: changes to the saved value require a server restart. The Preferences page surfaces an amber notice when the saved path differs from the running config, and the header's Restart icon glows when it detects the mismatch.

## What "valid" means

A folder is a valid Godot project root if it contains `project.godot` at the top level. The welcome screen + Preferences picker both hit the server's validation endpoint live as you pick / type.
